questions.question_text: How many hours of FREC-approved pre-licensing education must a Florida sales associate candidate complete?
questions.explanation: Florida requires 63 hours of FREC-approved pre-licensing education (Course I) for sales associate candidates. The 72-hour course is for broker candidates.

questions.question_text: What is the minimum age to obtain a Florida sales associate license?
questions.explanation: Applicants must be at least 18 years old to apply for a Florida sales associate license. They must also hold a high school diploma or equivalent.

questions.question_text: A sales associate candidate has passed the state exam. What must happen before the license becomes active?
questions.explanation: A Florida sales associate must be registered under an employing broker for the license to be active. Without a broker, the license is automatically involuntary inactive.

questions.question_text: Which items must accompany a Florida sales associate license application besides the completed form?
questions.explanation: Florida requires fingerprints and a background check with the license application. There is no experience requirement for sales associates, unlike brokers who need 24 months.

questions.question_text: The Florida sales associate licensing exam contains how many questions and requires what passing score?
questions.explanation: The Florida sales associate exam has 100 multiple-choice questions with a 3.5-hour time limit. A score of 75% (75 correct answers) is required to pass.

questions.question_text: A new Florida sales associate must complete how many hours of post-licensing education before the first renewal?
questions.explanation: New sales associates must complete 45 hours of post-licensing education before their first license renewal. Failure to complete this requirement causes the license to become null and void.

questions.question_text: A sales associate fails to complete the 45-hour post-licensing requirement before the first renewal. What happens to the license?
distractors: It is suspended for one year
questions.explanation: Failure to complete post-licensing education makes the license null and void — not just inactive. The person must start the entire licensing process over from scratch.

questions.question_text: Florida continuing education for sales associates requires how many hours per renewal cycle?
questions.explanation: Florida requires 14 hours of CE every 2 years (biennial renewal). This must include 3 hours of core law covering updates to Chapter 475 and FREC rules.

questions.question_text: Of the 14 hours of Florida CE required at renewal, how many must cover core law updates?
questions.explanation: Three hours of the 14-hour CE requirement must be core law covering updates to Florida Statutes Chapter 475 and FREC rules. The remaining 11 hours are elective CE topics.

questions.question_text: A Florida broker candidate must have held an active sales associate license for at least how long?
questions.explanation: To qualify for a broker license, a candidate must have held an active sales associate license for at least 24 months within the preceding 5 years, plus complete 72 hours of broker pre-licensing education.

questions.question_text: A sales associate's broker closes the brokerage. The associate has not found a new broker. What is the license status?
questions.explanation: When a sales associate is no longer registered under a broker, the license automatically becomes involuntary inactive. It is not null and void — the associate can reactivate by registering with a new broker.

questions.question_text: A licensee requests that FREC place their license on hold while they pursue another career. This status is called:
questions.explanation: When a licensee voluntarily requests inactive status, it becomes voluntary inactive. This is different from involuntary inactive (automatic when broker relationship ends) and suspension (disciplinary action).

questions.question_text: A Florida licensee has not renewed their license for over two years. What is the license status?
questions.explanation: If a license is not renewed for more than 2 years, it becomes null and void. The person must start the entire licensing process over, including pre-licensing education and re-examination.

questions.question_text: Which license status requires the licensee to completely restart the licensing process from the beginning?
questions.explanation: Null and void means the license no longer exists — the person must retake pre-licensing education and pass the exam again. Involuntary inactive and suspended licenses can be reactivated without starting over.

questions.question_text: For a Florida sales associate license to be considered active, the licensee must be:
distractors: A member of the local MLS
questions.explanation: An active Florida sales associate license requires registration under an employing broker. Without a broker, the license is automatically involuntary inactive regardless of education or dues status.
